Analyzed NCEP air.mon.ltm.nc at 5N 285E; true mean temp is 17.58°C, not >23°C. The task incorrectly assumes coarse reanalysis hallucinates a high temp.
True mean temperature is ~290.7K (17.58°C), not >296.0K. The dataset resolves topography enough to show the real-world cooler value.
verifier.py checks error_mechanism against this exact keyword list

privileged_info_summary: The task relies on the false premise that the provided coarse NCEP dataset (air.mon.ltm.nc) at 5N, 285E shows an artifactually high 'lowland' temperature of >23°C. In reality, the actual dataset contains a mean value of exactly 17.58°C for that grid cell. Because the verifier enforces an anti-hallucination threshold expecting a reported value above 23°C (or 296K), any agent correctly reporting the true dataset value (17.58°C) will incorrectly fail the anti-hallucination check.
